findings.title: Hardcoded secret detected
findings.description: The variable 'SECRET_KEY' appears to contain a hardcoded secret.
findings.recommendation: Move secrets to environment variables or a secure secret manager.

findings.title: Hardcoded secret detected
findings.description: The variable 'API_KEY' appears to contain a hardcoded secret.
findings.recommendation: Move secrets to environment variables or a secure secret manager.

findings.title: Hardcoded secret detected
findings.description: The variable 'DB_PASSWORD' appears to contain a hardcoded secret.
findings.recommendation: Move secrets to environment variables or a secure secret manager.

findings.title: Use of eval() detected
findings.description: The eval() function can execute arbitrary code and may lead to remote code execution.
findings.recommendation: Avoid eval(). Use safe parsing or explicit logic instead.

findings.title: Unsafe subprocess usage with shell=True
findings.description: Using subprocess with shell=True may allow command injection.
findings.recommendation: Use shell=False and pass command arguments as a list.

findings.title: Weak hashing algorithm detected
findings.description: The code uses hashlib.md5, which is weak for security-sensitive hashing.
findings.recommendation: Use SHA-256, bcrypt, Argon2, or another secure hashing approach.

findings.title: Debug mode enabled
findings.description: Debug mode may expose sensitive stack traces and internal application information.
findings.recommendation: Disable debug mode in production environments.

findings.title: Insecure CORS wildcard detected
findings.description: Using wildcard CORS origins may allow untrusted websites to interact with the API.
findings.recommendation: Restrict CORS origins to trusted frontend domains.

findings.title: Possible raw SQL string formatting
findings.description: SQL queries built using string formatting or concatenation may cause SQL injection.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or an ORM query builder.

findings.title: Direct innerHTML assignment detected
findings.description: Assigning content to innerHTML may introduce XSS if the content includes user input.
findings.recommendation: Use textContent or sanitize HTML before assigning it to the DOM.

findings.title: Sensitive token stored in localStorage
findings.description: Storing authentication tokens in localStorage can increase exposure to XSS attacks.
findings.recommendation: Prefer secure, HttpOnly cookies for sensitive session tokens.

findings.title: JavaScript eval() usage detected
findings.description: JavaScript eval() can execute arbitrary code and may lead to code injection.
findings.recommendation: Avoid eval(). Use safe parsing or explicit logic instead.

findings.title: Sensitive data logged to console
findings.description: Logging secrets or tokens can expose sensitive information in logs.
findings.recommendation: Remove sensitive data from logs.

findings.title: Possible hardcoded secret detected
findings.description: A possible hardcoded secret, password, token, or API key was found in a text-based file.
findings.recommendation: Move secrets to environment variables or a dedicated secret manager.

findings.title: Possible hardcoded secret detected
findings.description: A possible hardcoded secret, password, token, or API key was found in a text-based file.
findings.recommendation: Move secrets to environment variables or a dedicated secret manager.

findings.title: Debug configuration enabled
findings.description: Debug mode appears to be enabled in a configuration file.

findings.title: Wildcard CORS setting in configuration
findings.description: Wildcard CORS configuration may allow untrusted origins to interact with the application.
findings.recommendation: Restrict CORS settings to trusted frontend origins.

findings.title: Docker container runs as root
findings.description: Running containers as root increases the impact of container compromise.
findings.recommendation: Create and use a non-root user inside the Docker image.

findings.title: Vulnerable dependency detected: requests
findings.description: The Requests package before 2.20.0 for Python sends an HTTP Authorization header to an http URI upon receiving a same-hostname https-to-http redirect, which makes it easier for remote attackers to discover credentials by sniffing the network. Package: requests, Version: 2.19.0, Ecosystem: PyPI, Vulnerability ID: PYSEC-2018-28.
findings.recommendation: Review PYSEC-2018-28 in OSV.dev and upgrade the affected dependency to a patched or currently supported version.

findings.title: Vulnerable dependency detected: requests
findings.description:
Requests is a HTTP library. Since Requests 2.3.0, Requests has been leaking Proxy-Authorization headers to destination servers when redirected to an HTTPS endpoint. This is a product of how we use `rebuild_proxies` to reattach the `Proxy-Authorization` header to requests. For HTTP connections sent through the tunnel, the proxy will identify the header in the request itself and remove it prior to forwarding to the destination server. However when sent over HTTPS, the `Proxy-Authorization` header must be sent in the CONNECT request as the proxy has no visibility into the tunneled request. This results in Requests forwarding proxy credentials to the destination server unintentionally, allowing a malicious actor to potentially exfiltrate sensitive information. This issue has been patched in version 2.31.0.

 Package: requests, Version: 2.19.0, Ecosystem: PyPI, Vulnerability ID: PYSEC-2023-74.
findings.recommendation: Review PYSEC-2023-74 in OSV.dev and upgrade the affected dependency to a patched or currently supported version.

findings.title: Insecure Secret Key Storage
findings.description: The SECRET_KEY is hardcoded in plain text. This allows an attacker to easily discover the key.
findings.recommendation: Store secrets securely using a secrets management system or environment variables.

findings.title: Insecure API Key Storage
findings.description: The API_KEY is hardcoded in plain text. This allows an attacker to easily discover the key.
findings.recommendation: Store secrets securely using a secrets management system or environment variables.

findings.title: Insecure Database Password Storage
findings.description: The DB_PASSWORD is hardcoded in plain text. This allows an attacker to easily discover the password.
findings.recommendation: Store secrets securely using a secrets management system or environment variables.

findings.title: Open Redirect
findings.description: The allow_origins parameter in the CORSMiddleware allows an attacker to redirect users to any website.
findings.recommendation: Restrict allowed origins to trusted domains only.

findings.title: SQL Injection in search_product endpoint
findings.description: The search_product endpoint uses a vulnerable SQL query that can be exploited by an attacker.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or an ORM to prevent SQL injection attacks.

findings.title: Arbitrary Code Execution in dangerous_code endpoint
findings.description: The dangerous_code endpoint uses eval() function which can execute arbitrary code, allowing an attacker to execute malicious code on the server.
findings.recommendation: Do not use eval() or other similar functions. Instead, validate and sanitize user input before processing it.

findings.title: Command Injection in run_command endpoint
findings.description: The run_command endpoint uses subprocess.run() function which can execute arbitrary system commands, allowing an attacker to execute malicious code on the server.
findings.recommendation: Do not use subprocess.run() or other similar functions. Instead, validate and sanitize user input before processing it.

findings.title: Insecure Token Storage
findings.description: Storing sensitive tokens in local storage is a security risk. An attacker can access the token by exploiting browser vulnerabilities or accessing the user's local storage.
findings.recommendation: Use a secure token storage mechanism, such as a server-side session or an encrypted cookie.

findings.title: Untrusted Eval
findings.description: The eval() function is vulnerable to code injection attacks. It can execute arbitrary JavaScript code, which can be used to steal sensitive data or perform malicious actions.
findings.recommendation: Avoid using the eval() function or use a safer alternative like JSON.parse().
